panels.description: Medium close-up shot: Silver, charismatic and persuasive, gestures with a hooked hand, addressing unseen mutineers. His eyes are shrewd and determined. The background is a dim, crowded ship cabin interior, with faint outlines of other pirates. Lighting casts subtle shadows, highlighting his features.

bubbles.text: Now, that's about where we are, every mother's son of us, thanks to him, and Hands, and Anderson, and other ruination fools of you.

And if you want to know about number four, and that boy, why, shiver my timbers, isn't he a hostage? Are we a-going to waste a hostage? No, not us; he might be our last chance, and I shouldn't wonder. Kill that boy? Not me, mates!

panels.description: Medium shot: Silver leans forward intently, eyes narrowed, continuing his argument. He points towards George Merry and John, who are visible in the mid-ground looking skeptical; John with a bandage on his head, George with yellowish eyes. The cabin is dimly lit by a distant lantern.

bubbles.text: And number three? Ah, well, there's a deal to say to number three. Maybe you don't count it nothing to have a real college doctor to see you every day--you, John, with your head broken--or you, George Merry, that had the ague shakes upon you not six hours agone, and has your eyes the colour of lemon peel to this same moment on the clock? And maybe, perhaps, you didn't know there was a consort coming either? But there is, and not so long till then; and we'll see who'll be glad to have a hostage when it comes to that.

And as for number two, and why I made a bargain--well, you came crawling on your knees to me to make it--on your knees you came, you was that downhearted--and you'd have starved too if I hadn't--but that's a trifle! You look there--that's why!

panels.description: Dramatic low-angle medium shot: Silver's extended hand, framed by the bottom of the panel, with a yellow, tattered chart featuring three prominent red crosses fluttering as it falls to the dusty wooden floor. The chart is in sharp focus. Mutineers' boots and lower legs are visible in the background, their attention drawn to the falling paper. Sharp, dramatic shadows are cast by an unseen light source.

bubbles.text: And he cast down upon the floor a paper that I instantly recognized--none other than the chart on yellow paper, with the three red crosses, that I had found in the oilcloth at the bottom of the captain's chest.

panels.description: Overhead wide shot: A chaotic tangle of mutineers' hands and arms, like a pack of starving animals, clawing and tearing at each other on the dirty wooden floor, desperately trying to grab the treasure chart. Their faces, visible from above, are distorted with greedy excitement and primal joy. The ship's messy cabin is strewn with debris. Dynamic and energetic lines emphasize their frantic movements.

bubbles.text: Why the doctor had given it to him was more than I could fancy. But if it were inexplicable to me, the appearance of the chart was incredible to the surviving mutineers.

They leaped upon it like cats upon a mouse. It went from hand to hand, one tearing it from another; and by the oaths and the cries and the childish laughter with which they accompanied their examination, you would have thought, not only they were fingering the very gold, but were at sea with it, besides, in safety.

panels.description: Dynamic medium-close shot: Silver, red-faced and furious, veins bulging on his neck, leans heavily against a wooden bulkhead with one hand for support. His eyes are blazing with rage as he shouts down at George Merry, whose nervous, intimidated face is partially visible in the foreground. Harsh, dramatic lighting from a lamp emphasizes Silver's anger and the tense atmosphere.

bubbles.text: Mighty pretty. But how are we to get away with it, and us no ship.

Now I give you warning, George. One more word of your sauce, and I'll call you down and fight you. How? Why, how do I know? You had ought to tell me that--you and the rest, that lost me my schooner, with your interference, burn you! But not you, you can't; you hain't got the invention of a cockroach. But civil you can speak, and shall, George Merry, you may lay to that.

panels.description: Medium wide shot: Morgan, a grizzled old pirate, stands beside Silver, nodding in agreement. Around them, the rest of the mutineers raise their fists and cheer, their faces alight with renewed loyalty and excitement. Silver, now composed, offers a cunning, triumphant smirk, having successfully manipulated the situation. Cluttered ship cabin background.

bubbles.text: Fair! I reckon so. You lost the ship; I found the treasure. Who's the better man at that? And now I resign, by thunder! Elect whom you please to be your cap'n now; I'm done with it.
Silver! Barbecue forever! Barbecue for cap'n!

panels.description: Medium shot: Silver, calm and collected, holds up the tattered 'black spot' paper, a sly smirk playing on his lips, then casually tosses it towards Jim Hawkins, whose outstretched hand is visible catching it. Dick, nearby, looks visibly uneasy, scratching his head. The background is a detailed view of the ship's rustic interior with hanging lanterns providing a soft, focused light.

bubbles.text: So that's the toon, is it? George, I reckon you'll have to wait another turn, friend; and lucky for you as I'm not a revengeful man. But that was never my way. And now, shipmates, this black spot? 'Tain't much good now, is it? Dick's crossed his luck and spoiled his Bible, and that's about all.
It'll do to kiss the book on still, won't it? A Bible with a bit cut out! Not it. It don't bind no more'n a ballad-book.
Don't it, though? Well, I reckon that's worth having too.
Here, Jim--here's a curiosity for you,

It was around about the size of a crown piece. One side was blank, for it had been the last leaf; the other contained a verse or two of Revelation--these words among the rest, which struck sharply home upon my mind: "Without are dogs and murderers." The printed side had been blackened with wood ash, which already began to come off and soil my fingers; on the blank side had been written with the same material the one word "Depposed." I have that curiosity beside me at this moment, but not a trace of writing now remains beyond a single scratch, such as a man might make with his thumb-nail."

dominant_emotion: mounting tension and eventual triumph, ending with a moment of curiosity
